response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and systems software developer with strong web, testing, and open-source credentials, but the resume shows no experience in SoC or IP modeling, AI accelerators, GPU architecture, or deep-learning workload optimization — the core requirements of this principal-level hardware performance role. His performance experience is at the application/service layer (low-latency DLang microservices), not at the hardware-software co-design or silicon architecture level, making this a fundamental domain mismatch despite ample years of experience.
strengths: Genuine low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput microservices in DLang)
Broad polyglot background including C/C++ work on engineering tooling at National Instruments
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source libraries, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware co-design experience
No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture background
